content:
Learn the Right Balance Between Signals and RxJS to Write Faster, Cleaner Angular Applications

5 min read
Just now

--

Angular developers have relied on RxJS Observables for years to build reactive applications. Whether it was handling HTTP requests, managing component state, or sharing data between services, Observables became the default solution for almost every reactive problem.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

source-internetIf Not a Member READ FROM HERE
Then Angular introduced Signals, and many developers immediately asked the same question:
“Should I stop using RxJS?”
The short answer is no.
Signals are not a replacement for RxJS. Instead, they solve a different set of problems — and in many cases, they solve them more simply. The real improvement comes from understanding when to use Signals and when Observables are still the better tool.
If you’ve been using Observables for everything, this article will help you rethink your approach and build Angular applications that are easier to read, maintain, and optimize.
Why Observables Became the Default
